issue_title: TOML emitter is wrong?
issue_body:
This seems incorrect:

``` python
>>> import toml
>>> m = {'a': {}, 'b': {}}
>>> toml.dumps(m)
''
```

I would have expected:

```
[a]

[b]

```

I'm not super familiar with all the edge cases of the TOML format so perhaps this is expected, but it seems weird to say a Map of Empty Map's becomes equivalent to empty string.

issue_title: TaskGroup closes prematurely
issue_body:
# Symptom
When running the following code (python 3.6.3, curio 0.8):

```python
import curio


async def level1():

    async def level2(task_group):

        async def level3():
            print("hello world")

        await task_group.spawn(level3)

    async with curio.TaskGroup() as task_group:
        await task_group.spawn(level2, task_group)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    curio.run(level1)
```

I'm getting:

```
Task 3 crashed
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/kernel.py", line 826, in _run_coro
    trap = current._send(current.next_value)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 96, in _task_runner
    return await coro
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/bug.py", line 11, in level2
    await task_group.spawn(level3)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 336, in spawn
    raise RuntimeError('Task group is closed')
RuntimeError: Task group is closed
Task 2 crashed
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/kernel.py", line 826, in _run_coro
    trap = current._send(current.next_value)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 96, in _task_runner
    return await coro
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/bug.py", line 14, in level1
    await task_group.spawn(level2, task_group)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 446, in __aexit__
    await self.join(wait=self._wait)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 435, in join
    raise TaskGroupError(exceptional)
curio.errors.TaskGroupError: TaskGroupError(RuntimeError)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/kernel.py", line 826, in _run_coro
    trap = current._send(current.next_value)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 96, in _task_runner
    return await coro
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/bug.py", line 14, in level1
    await task_group.spawn(level2, task_group)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 446, in __aexit__
    await self.join(wait=self._wait)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/task.py", line 435, in join
    raise TaskGroupError(exceptional)
curio.errors.TaskGroupError: TaskGroupError(RuntimeError)

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/bug.py", line 18, in <module>
    curio.run(level1)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/kernel.py", line 877, in run
    return kernel.run(corofunc, *args, timeout=timeout)
  File "/Users/jon/code/python/hello-curio/lib/python3.6/site-packages/curio/kernel.py", line 214, in run
    raise TaskError('Task Crashed') from ret_exc
curio.errors.TaskError: Task Crashed
```

# Analysis 
The problem seems to be that `task_group` is marked closed as soon as execution in `level1` reaches the `async with` block end, which doesn't take into account that tasks under the `task_group` may continue spawning additional tasks under `task_group`.

# Why pass a TaskGroup to a spawned task?
Because otherwise it's impossible to return a value and have tasks keep running in the background.
Consider the following code (which also fails):

```python
import curio


async def level1():

    async def level2(task_group):
        queue = curio.Queue()

        async def level3():

            async def level4():
                message = await queue.get()
                print(message)

            await task_group.spawn(level4)

        await task_group.spawn(level3)

        return queue

    async with curio.TaskGroup() as task_group:
        queue = await level2(task_group)
        await queue.put("hello world")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    curio.run(level1)
```

Right now I'm working around this by suppling the queue from outside as a parameter - but this is ugly IMO.

issue_title: Error when setting and item where a dictionary is nested in a list
issue_body:
here: https://github.com/gmr/flatdict/blob/3.0.0/flatdict.py#L402 it assumes that if a nested object is an array, the tail of the path needs to be an int. but if the path includes other path elements after the list index, it will fail

issue_title: Add formatting code for compact.
issue_body:
Now that #224 is merged (thanks @ryanpdwyer ), it might be a good idea to provide a formatting code to provide a compact display. A few implementation notes:
- combining with abbreviation code (`~`) should be possible.
- combining with latex, html, pretty (`L`, `H`, `P`) should be possible.
- most not change the quantity itself.

The proposed syntax is using the letter `C` or the symbol `#`
Good thing about `C`: Compact starts with C

Good thing about `#` (or other symbol): it keeps the difference between letters (L/H/P) and symbols (~, #). The first are interchangeable but not mixable, the second are modifiers.

issue_title: Algebraic Normal Form
Provide a command-line client for pyowm
issue_body:
It would be good to have a CLI command built out of PyOWM exposing at least a subset of the lib features.

Reasons:
  - scripting would be easier
  - usable also by a non-Python audience

Raw requirements (*more to add*):
  - output is pure text and therefore greppable
  - command is POSIX compliant
  - no PyOWM nor PYthon knowledge expected in order to run the command
  - installed via `pip install pyowm`

A few ideas on the usage:
```shell
$ pyowm -V    # prints versions
$ pyowm -h    # prints help
               -config $CONFIGFILE    # read config (api key, subscription type, ... from file)
               -apikey $APIKEY           # only use api key
$ pyowm forecast -frequency="daily" -on="Milan,it"  # get daily forecast on Milan, IT
```

issue_title: [Feature Request] Mean Square Loss Function
issue_body:
Hello,

It will be great if Mean Square Loss was part of your default library (For Linear Regression).
I know you can write your own loss layers but this is a very commonly used feature for DNN so it will be fantastic if it could be included by default.

Can you give a brief description of how the gradients are calculated in your API for the loss layer class?
For Mean Square Loss, I am assuming the local gradient should be calculated something like this:
https://github.com/nyanp/tiny-cnn/blob/master/tiny_cnn/lossfunctions/loss_function.h (under mse)

How do we go about implementing Mean Square Loss ourselves in your API? I read your docs about it but it wasn't clear about the gradient calculation portion.

Thanks!

issue_title: Merge metadata when merging ontologies
issue_body:
When merging ontologies, metadata should be merged too.

Tags with cardinality **any** should be simply merged together, and those with `zero or one` or `one` should be treated separalety:

* `format-version`: greatest of the two
* `data-version`: greatest of the two
* `date`: date of the merge
* `saved-by`: None
* `auto-generated-by`: None (or `pronto vX.X.X` itself)
* ` default-relationship-id-prefix` : the one of the merge-receiving ontology

issue_title: Finnish fixed holidays are moved to the next monday if they occur on the weekend
issue_body:
I'm using calendra 1.1.3 on Python 2.7.8. I'm using the Finnish holiday calendar and have noticed a problem with it.

When using the Finnish calendar, fixed holidays that occur on a weekend are moved to the next working day. Example, the Finnish Independence Day that is the 6th of December:

```
In [3]: holiday_calendar.is_observed_holiday(date(2014, 12, 8))                                                       
Out[3]: True

In [4]: holiday_calendar.is_observed_holiday(date(2014, 12, 6))                                                       
Out[4]: False

In [7]: holiday_calendar.get_observed_date(date(2014, 12, 6))                                                         
Out[7]: datetime.date(2014, 12, 8)
```

This also happens with the Labour Day (1st of May). But in Finland the day off is not transferred to the next monday if the day occurs on the weekend. I can't find a source on it though, but you can check other calendars such as http://www.timeanddate.com/holidays/finland/.

This issue can be fixed by setting an `observance_shift = dict()` for the Finnish calendar, though I arrived at the value of `dict()` just by random testing (I don't know the format) and I don't know if it has any adverse effects.

I'll try to fix it later.

issue_title: wrong hanling of bit offset for embeded channels in mdf version 3
issue_body:
Using the test file and plotting _Fsf_pres_sens_rla_

it should be:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20952040/29494482-f4db3bfe-85b3-11e7-8b47-6a910c781411.png)

but mdfreader plots:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20952040/29494494-1df2eabe-85b4-11e7-86d0-887743d7614c.png)

I think the error comes from the fact that when loading the record info, the parent channel is a 16 bit channel, and this channel has a bit offset of 8 relative to the parent channel, while in the conversion the bit offset is computed from the offset in the channel group record , resulting in a bit offset of 0.

issue_title: filter_kwargs and python 3.5 compatibility
issue_body:
`util.filter_kwargs` uses the inspect module's `getargspec` function [here](https://github.com/craffel/mir_eval/blob/master/mir_eval/util.py#L715), which is deprecated in python 3.5.

In python 3, this has been replaced with the `signature` function (see [here](https://docs.python.org/3/library/inspect.html#inspect.signature)), which sadly doesn't work on py2.

You might be able to side-step this by using `six.get_function_defaults()`.

Alternatievly, our buddies at sklearn have a fix for this [here](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/commit/6966881328c256bbef37752cce23bbac8e23fb27).

issue_title: AttributeError thrown when native _decimal module is not available
issue_body:
When importing the `decimal` module, Jedi throws an AttributeError if the native `_decimal` module is not available, instead of falling back to `_pydecimal` by following the ImportError except clause. The native module is only available if the external `mpdecimal` library is installed. I came across this when my auto-completer started throwing exceptions everywhere while editing a file that contained `from decimal import Decimal` at the top. :smile:

This is an example of what happens when the native module is not available:
```python
Python 3.5.1 (default, Mar  3 2016, 09:29:07)

Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

>>> jedi.Script(source='from decimal import ').completions()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/jedi/api/__init__.py", line 188, in completions
    completion_names = get_completions(user_stmt, b)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/jedi/api/__init__.py", line 150, in get_completions
    completion_names = i.completion_names(self._evaluator, only_modules)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/jedi/evaluate/imports.py", line 414, in completion_names
    for names_dict in scope.names_dicts(search_global=False):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/jedi/evaluate/representation.py", line 720, in names_dicts

Hi there,

Trying to use a Python VirtualEnv with nodeenv to keep installation dependencies within a single folder.

I followed the steps as best as I can, but there seems to be a fatal confusion about what folders contain what. For example if I run the following from a clean start...

...then I get the following error, which prevents me from installing this simple bootstrap template repo (very few dependencies) and seems to come from the fact that npm is actually trying to access a global folder, not the virtualenv one ...

issue_title: NetNS leaks open unix sockets until the open file limit is reached
issue_body:
This is possibly related to #167, but this issue appears version 0.3.14 and also in 0.3.4, where NSPopen still didn't exist, so I think it is ok to report this particular case. It can be easily reproduced by running the following script:

```python
import time
from pyroute2 import NetNS
from pyroute2 import netns as netnsmod

i = 0
while True:
    nsid = 'testns_%s' % i
    ns = NetNS(nsid)
    time.sleep(5)
    ns.close()
    netnsmod.remove(nsid)
    time.sleep(70)  # Intentionally big to let the namespace resources be properly closed
    i = i + 1
```

Looking at the open unix sockets for the process one can see the open unix sockets (I assume they correspond to the communication channel with the namespace server):

```bash
$ watch -n 1 "ss -p | egrep 'Netid|14381'"
Netid  State      Recv-Q Send-Q   Local Address:Port       Peer Address:Port
u_str  ESTAB      0      0                    * 1142354               * 1142353 users:(("python",14386,8),("python",14381,8))
u_str  ESTAB      0      0                    * 1142352               * 1142351 users:(("python",14386,6),("python",14381,6))
u_str  ESTAB      0      0                    * 1142353               * 1142354 users:(("python",14386,7),("python",14381,7))
u_str  ESTAB      0      0                    * 1142351               * 1142352 users:(("python",14386,5),("python",14381,5))
...
```

During the first 5 seconds sleep, before closing the namespace, the unix sockets appear open and connected (both ends of the socket are shown), but after the namespace is closed and removed, during the second wait, the unix sockets are still alive but the output only shows one extreme of the socket (it is actually disconnected, but still alive).

Those sockets are leaked and never closed, and each iteration creates new sockets when each new namespace is created. The program will eventually start failing with `Too many open files` errors, which makes it a blocker issue when it comes to use the network namespace support in production.

issue_title: python 3.4+ asyncio support
issue_body:
Hi,

If I understand correctly, the only way to make pyroute2 behave with asyncio for now is to run ipdb in an [executor](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html?highlight=run_in_executor#executor) thread. This makes it hard to have interaction with non blocking code outside of the thread.

I wonder if that would be possible to add support for non blocking I/O using asyncio coroutines. I'm not sure if all low level socket API is available with asyncio sockets.

https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html#creating-connections

I might try to have a shot at this, but I'd like some guidance on what has to be changed. I saw a lot of threading locks and other blocking synchronization routines which do not live well with asyncio. Maybe this would require splitting some parts of the code and have 2 versions of `IPRoute` and `IPDB`.

    import signal
    
    def sigterm_handler(signo, frame):

    import signal

    consumer = KafkaConsumer('my-topic')
    for msg in consumer:

issue_title: Python 3.7 support
issue_body:
**```async```** is a reserved keyword in Python 3.7.
**```async```** is used a bit in kafka-python, so this currently breaks Python 3.7 compatibility. See e.g. ```producer/base.py```

Using something like **```async_```** would be fine though, so quickest way to resolve this would be just to rename a few things from **```async```** to **```async_```** or something like that.

Thanks!

issue_title: Add existing=None argument to sync
Option for slow queries
issue_body:
Of course we should all have indexes for things we query often. However, there are sometimes things that are not queried often, and so we shouldn't be building and updating an index for it all the time. But when it's time to walk the database for non-critical purposes (backup, debug, daily reports, etc), it seems like ROM could still help.

My example is the `path` column in #72... If I want a unique list of all paths, there is no way to express that using ROM. However, I think a `SORT` command could be constructed that returns the results fairly quickly.

Similarly, I might want to sort on a column occasionally, but not index it. `SORT` can do that... Or maybe count a value.

I'm not at all sure what the UI for this feature would look like, but at the end of the day it feels like a missing piece. In some ways, it's a mostly a wrapper for SORT, but there may be some cases where it can optimize based on what indexes you have already.

issue_title: icon-white class within bootstrap 3 theme conflicts with icomoon CSS selector
issue_body:
Icomoon (https://icomoon.io/#docs) which provides numerous glyphicons to bootstrap includes on its CSS files a wide scope selector [class*="icon-"]. 

When using tablesorter's bootstrap theme the icomoon selector will conflict with the icon-white class proposed on tablesorter documentation (https://mottie.github.io/tablesorter/docs/example-option-theme-bootstrap-v3.html), and the icon will be not properly presented.

  $.tablesorter.themes.bootstrap = {
 ...
    icons        : '', // **add "icon-white"** to make them white; this icon class is added to the <i> in the header

}

issue_title: [Security issue] Resource.meta should be immutable
issue_body:
There is a bug here https://github.com/klen/flask-restler/blob/develop/flask_restler/mongo.py#L228
```python
        if self.meta.aggregate:
            self.meta.aggregate = [p for p in self.meta.aggregate if '$sort' not in p]
            self.meta.aggregate.append({'$sort': sorting})
```

self.meta is a class field thus it's value shared among all instances of the `Resource` class.  

Proposal:
1. Make `self.meta` immutable
2. Add `self.aggregate` field to the `MongoResource` class and copy `self.meta.aggregate` value to it on instantiation.

Example pseudo-code:
```python
class  MongoResource(Resource):
    def __init__(self, collection):
        self.aggregate = self.meta.aggregete.copy()
```

issue_title: UnicodeDecodeError on table value functions with non ASCII column names
issue_body:
This might be related to the general issue #112, but as it's a very specific case with exampel code and infrastrcuture details, I decided to open a separate issue:

When running an SQL query that accesses a table value function (TVF) with non ASCII characters in the column name (e.g. `ä`), pyodbc fails with:

> UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xe4 in position 1: invalid continuation byte

To reproduce this, first create a trivial table value function TVF_temp that returns a single value with the heading "Waehrung" (ASCIIfied German for "currency"):

``` sql
use SomeDatabase;

if object_id('SomeDatabase.dbo.TVF_temp') is not null
    drop function dbo.TVF_temp;
go

create function dbo.TVF_temp(@Waehrung char(1)) returns table as return
select @Waehrung as [Waehrung];
go
```

Using SQL Server Management Studio, it is possible to query this function:

``` sql
select * from dbo.TVF_temp('€');
```

As expected, the result is:

```
Waehrung
--------
€

(1 row(s) affected)
```

Now run the same query using Python and pyodbc (replace `SomeServer` and `SomeDatabase` by approriate values and possibly add additonal connection parameters):

``` python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import logging
import pyodbc
from contextlib import closing

# Specify connection.
server = 'SomeServer'
database = 'SomeDatabase'
connection_string = 'DRIVER={{SQL Server}};SERVER={server};DATABASE={database}'.format(server=server, database=database)
query_statement = "select * from dbo.TVF_temp('€')"

# Setup logging.
_log = logging.getLogger('temp')
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

_log.info('connecting to %s', connection_string)
with pyodbc.connect(connection_string) as connection:
    with closing(connection.cursor()) as cursor:
        _log.info
        try:
            for row in cursor.execute(query_statement):
                _log.info('row=%s', row)
        except UnicodeDecodeError as error:
            _log.error('error.args=%s', error.args)
            raise
_log.info('done')
```

With Python 3.5, the output is as expected:

```
INFO:temp:connecting to DRIVER={SQL Server};SERVER=...;DATABASE=...
INFO:temp:row = ('€', )
INFO:temp:done
```

This is exactly what was desired, so passing data and retrieving results with Unicode characters works fine.

Now change the column name `[Waehrung]` to `[Währung]`:

``` sql
create function dbo.TVF_temp(@Waehrung char(1)) returns table as return
select @Waehrung as [Währung];
go
```

In SQL Server Management Studio this still yields the expected result, with a nice "ä" in the Heading:

```
Währung
-------
€

(1 row(s) affected)
```

Running the Python code again though fails with:

```
INFO:temp:connecting to DRIVER={SQL Server};SERVER=...;DATABASE=...
ERROR:temp:error.args=('utf-8', b'W\xe4hrung', 1, 2, 'invalid continuation byte')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Temp\pyodbc_unicode_error.py", line 23, in <module>
    for row in cursor.execute(query_statement):
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xe4 in position 1: invalid continuation byte
```

In error.args you can see that "Währung" is encoded using CP1252 (b'W\xe4hrung'), nevertheless pyodbc attempts to decode it as UTF-8.

Adding CHARSET=UTF-8 to the connection string does not change this.

When researching the topic, I found the following ODBC Unicode tutorial: ​https://www.progress.com/odbc/resources/tutorials/unicode/non-unicode. According to this it seems that on Unix ODBC indeeed uses UTF-8 while under Windows it's either CP1252 or UTF16/UCS2.

Interestingly, running the above code with Python 2.6 yields the following result:

```
INFO:temp:connecting to DRIVER={SQL Server};SERVER=...;DATABASE=...
INFO:temp:row = ('\x80', )
INFO:temp:done
```

So it works, but Python just uses a byte string to represent the result, effectively leaving the CP1252 Euro Sign at 0x80 instead of translating it to the Unicode code point 0x20ac. I only added this example for comparison, I don't expect any fixes or changes for the dated pyodbc version we were using with Python 2.

So seemingly proper Unicode translation happens for queries sent, parameters specified and results transmitted over ODBC. For indirectly quering columns names used by table value functions, no translation takes placed and things are left to the mercy of Python default encoding. Which in case of Python 3's default result in a UnicodeDecodeError when attempting to decode CP1252 using UTF-8.

Here are the details on the infrastructure used:

Python
- Python 3.5.1 (via Anaconda 3), pyodbc 3.0.10 
- Python 2.6.6, pyodbc 2.1.11 

MS SQL Server properties: 
- Version: 11.0.56133.0 
- Language: English 
- Server collation: Latin_1_General_CI_AS 
- Operating System: Windows NT 6.1 (7601) 
- Platform: NT x64 

ODBC Driver 1: 
- Name: SQL Server 
- Version: 6.01.7601.17514 
- File: SQLSRV32.DDL 

ODBC Driver 2 (same result): 
- SQL Server Native Client 11.0 
- Version: 2011.110.3000.00 
- SQLNCLI11.DDL

issue_title: ubuntu:16.06 -> new msodbc driver={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server} => fail to restart
issue_body:
I did a `sudo apt update; sudo apt upgrade` and I got a new msodbcsql driver:
v13 was out and in with v17:

Our test containers have the following mssql config:

```
RUN /usr/bin/curl https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | apt-key add - && \
    /usr/bin/curl https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/16.04/prod.list > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mssqlrelease.list && \
    apt-get update && \
    ACCEPT_EULA=Y apt-get install msodbcsql mssql-tools unixodbc-dev -y && \
```

```
$ dpkg-query -l | grep msodbcsql
ii  msodbcsql                         17.0.1.1-1                                 amd64        ODBC Driver for Microsoft(R) SQL Server(R)
$ dpkg-query -L msodbcsql | grep .ini
/opt/microsoft/msodbcsql/etc/odbcinst.ini
$ cat /opt/microsoft/msodbcsql/etc/odbcinst.ini
[ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server]
Description=Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server
Driver=/opt/microsoft/msodbcsql/lib64/libmsodbcsql-17.0.so.1.1 
```
But when the system came up again, pyodbc complained missing driver, This is naturally because the
the version number is in the driver, so it was only to change it in my connectors *ini file:
driver = {ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server}

But my question is how can I prevent this from happening in production next time
MS decides to upgrade their driver?

Thanks

issue_title: Rows with very long strings are not returned without throwing an exception
issue_body:
### Environment

To diagnose, we usually need to know the following, including version numbers.  On Windows, be
sure to specify 32-bit Python or 64-bit:

- Python: 3.5.4
- pyodbc: 3.0.10
- OS: Windows 7
- DB: Microsoft SQL Server 14.0.3022.28
- driver: ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server

### Issue

Repro/observed behavior:
Execute some sql to create a table and insert some test data:
```
CREATE TABLE mytest (
	col1 varchar(max) NULL,
	bodytext varchar(max) NULL
)

INSERT INTO mytest (col1, bodytext)
VALUES ('thing1', 'short text')

INSERT INTO rleetest (col1, bodytext)
VALUES ('thing2', 'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Morbi gravida leo a purus lacinia ullamcorper. Maecenas nec erat ornare, pulvinar mi ac, tristique massa. Nullam imperdiet, neque at pellentesque vulputate, erat nulla congue felis, tristique porta purus augue eget lacus. Vivamus ultricies ex leo, et placerat sem imperdiet sed. In pellentesque nunc tortor, id tempor arcu tempus in. Nulla rutrum felis quis imperdiet tincidunt. Aliquam sodales erat rutrum lectus tempor tempor. Ut quis tincidunt ligula. Interdum et malesuada fames ac ante ipsum primis in faucibus. Donec aliquet, erat vitae dignissim efficitur, ante nunc molestie ipsum, sed sollicitudin neque erat ultricies magna. Pellentesque nisi metus, tempor at congue ut, ullamcorper ornare quam. Curabitur rhoncus risus malesuada feugiat vulputate. Aliquam suscipit nisl id metus facilisis porttitor. Cras venenatis auctor tempus. Nunc vel velit id velit laoreet dictum ac ut enim. Sed laoreet nunc varius nibh porta, sed congue neque auctor.

Quisque eros mauris, pulvinar ac ex in, mattis finibus purus. Nulla placerat eros at est semper porttitor. Suspendisse finibus leo vel porttitor aliquam. Mauris at sagittis lectus, vitae imperdiet mi. Suspendisse viverra nunc nec libero vehicula, accumsan facilisis nunc tempor. Morbi bibendum vel sem quis mollis. Nulla sit amet orci orci. Maecenas vel tortor ipsum. Nam venenatis nunc a urna tristique iaculis.

[snipped, this should be 40000 bytes of lorem ipsum from https://www.lipsum.com/]')
```

Now, from python, run:
```
import pyodbc
connstr = """DRIVER={ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server};SERVER=myservername;DATABASE=mydatabasename;Trusted_Connection=Yes;"""
cursor = pyodbc.connect(connstr).cursor()

cursor.execute('select count(*) from mytest')
cursor.fetchone()  # returns 2, as expected

cursor.execute('select top 10 * from rleetest')
cursor.fetchall()  # returns just one row, e.g. [('thing1', 'short text')]
```

Expected behavior:
`cursor.fetchall()` should return both rows (or at least throw an exception!)

issue_title: Using callback in textbox - how?
issue_body:
Hi,

Following advice, I've been trying to refine my code to pass commands into easygui, which primarily helps with debugging. It's been going well, on balance.

However, I'm having difficulty with the callback function of the textbox.

I've read the following and I've failed to implement it functionally:

[http://easygui.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html](url)

Here is says that:

```
callback: function
if set, this function will be called when OK is pressed

None
If cancel is pressed
```

If I have callback set as follows, it does nothing, as expected:

```
if easygui.ynbox(msg_a, title_a):
    pass
    easygui.textbox(
        msg=msg_b, title=title_b, text=ans_y_filecont, callback=None)
else:
    easygui.textbox(
        msg=msg_b, title=title_c, text=ans_n_filecont, callback=None)
```

If I write my code as follows, it just quits (following issue of quit() command) as soon as the textbox is presented on screen, so the textbox immediately disappears:

```
callback_quit = quit()
if easygui.ynbox(msg_a, title_a):
    pass
    easygui.textbox(
        msg=msg_b, title=title_b, text=ans_y_filecont, callback=callback_quit)
else:
    easygui.textbox(
        msg=msg_b, title=title_c, text=ans_n_filecont, callback=callback_quit)
```

At least I know quit() is working, but why isn't it waiting until the callback on CANCEL button is pressed?

Am I implementing the callback function incorrectly?

Is there another function I should be calling with callback?

Thanks.

issue_title: Improve browser reload behavior
issue_body:
As noted by @adamcik and others, a browser reload may mess up the library (and also playlist) view, since the currently displayed directory will become the new "root", with no "back" button being shown.
Although this shouldn't be a problem on a mobile devices (and therefore is unlikely to be addressed in ionic), it's still a nuisance when testing.
A simple workaround might be to automatically "redirect" to the library (or playlists) root when no `backView` is available in the current tab's history stack. This could be checked in an `$ionicView.loaded` event handler, for example (would be nice to do this in a state `resolve`, but the view and its back view are not available there).
Care must be taken regarding view caching, but since all "resolve" views are not cached for now, this shouldn't be much of an issue.

issue_title: Uniformize and standardize 'format' option
issue_body:
Looking at the current modules, I found that the option allowing users
to define a custom output format is not consistent or not present. One
thing that made me like i3status was the idea that no matter the module,
it is the `format` option that is used to customize its output. I
think it would be nice to uniformize existant modules around this idea
and to document this as the way to go for future modules.

Beside the uniformization, it offers more flexibility for users : take
for example a simple module like
[whoami](https://github.com/Ultrabug/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/whoami.py).
It actually only offers the possibility to display the username of the
user running py3status. No formatting of the output is possible.
Wouldn't it be nice if it defined a `format = "{username}"` in the
configuration ? It would allow the following :

* Still the same default behavior
* Definable by users in i3status.conf, to add simple things sur as
  prefix or suffix (which are definitely handy and pretty when used with
  UTF8 icons)
* By using directly `{username}` and making it available to the user,
  there isn't more parsing to do on the module side. Just return
  `format.format(username = username)` as response and it's done and
  working for both default value and possible user custom values

Details for existing modules (OK means it does it the way I described as
ideal; KO means the output is absolutely not configurable atm; links are
on the concerned lines) :

* [aws_bill](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/aws_bill.py#L38)
  : KO; no output customization available
* [battery_level](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/battery_level.py#L40)
  has a `format` option but with unamed parameters, which is probably
  not intuitive for users that would want to redefine it themselves
* [bitcoin_price](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/bitcoin_price.py#L38)
  KO; doesn't have any output configuration
* [bluetooth](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/bluetooth.py#L38)
  **OK**
* [clementine](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/clementine.py#L16)
  KO; offers absolutely no output costomization, would probably greatly
  benefit from it and would be easily adaptable
* [dpms](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/dpms.py#L16)
  : KO; no customization, easy to add
* [glpi](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/glpi.py#L24)
  KO; would benefit from it
* [imap](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/imap.py#L33)
  **OK**
* [keyboard_layout](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/keyboard_layout.py#L54)
  KO; has no output config
* [mpd_status](https://github.com/Ultrabug/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/mpd_status.py#L47)
  **OK**
* [net_rate](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/net_rate.py#L40)
  **OK**
* [netdata](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/netdata.py#L49)
  doesn't have a configurable output but already uses .format strings
* [ns_checker](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/ns_checker.py#L27)
  KO; satisfies itself by only displaying a defined output with "NOK" or
  "OK", and would benefit from this uniformization
* [nvidia_temp](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/nvidia_temp.py#L35)
  offers customization of prefix and units (suffix) : would benefit from
  this standardization
* [online_status](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/online_status.py#L29)
  allows output customization with `format_offline` and `format_online`
  as options. Having the `format_online` named `format` would be more
  intuitive and consistent
* [pingdom](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/pingdom.py#L29)
  KO; doesn't allow any output customization and would benefit from a
  `format` option
* [player_control](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/player_control.py#L40)
  already have customizable icons, but would probably be improved by a
  `format` for people who want to add a prefix such as an UTF8 music
  note or that kind of stuff
* [pomodoro](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/pomodoro.py#L36)
  looks a bit tricky to adapt to this suggestion
* [rate_counter](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/rate_counter.py#L20)
  KO; has a completely hardcoded output
* [scratchpad_counter](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/scratchpad_counter.py#L29)
  uses a .format string and document what `{}` will be replaced for, but
  a named argument would make it uniformized with the other modules
* [screenshot](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/screenshot.py#L31)
  KO but seems to have a really variable output depending on clicks,
  maybe not adaptable
* [spaceapi](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/spaceapi.py#L33)
  has a partially customizable output, but would need a renaming. The
  `%H` instead of strings formatted with `{x}` is probably a limitation
  imposed by datetime
* [spotify](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/spotify.py#L34)
  **OK**; does everything right
* [sysdata](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/sysdata.py#L77)
  KO
* [vnstat](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/vnstat.py#L65)
  **OK** : does it exactly as suggested
* [weather_yahoo](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/weather_yahoo.py#L26)
  KO
* [whatismyip](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/whatismyip.py#L30)
  looks **OK**
* [whoami](https://github.com/Ultrabug/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/whoami.py#L16)
  doesn't offer any way to customize the output
* [window_title](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/window_title.py#L38)
  KO; but seems tricky to adapt
* [xrandr](https://github.com/Horgix/py3status/blob/master/py3status/modules/xrandr.py#L62)
  KO

I took the time to write such a enhancement suggestion because it
involves changing parameter names for several modules, which would not
be compatible with existing configurations. I guess it needs discussion,
and will depend on what @Ultrabug wants for this project :)

issue_title: Missing way to delete the clipboard after pastes
issue_body:
You should be able to instruct the clipboard to delete its content after some number of pastes - typically 1.

To my understanding, `xclip` allows a [`--loop` option](https://linux.die.net/man/1/xclip) that let you specifiy the number of pastes to allow before deleting the clipboard. (Not sure though, I'm not on a Linux box right now).

Use case of this feature: an application that produces secrets might want to copy the secret directly into the clipboard instead of ask the user to copy - for usability reasons. Yet, leaving the content after it has been consumed sounds bad.

Possible API:
```
pyperclip.copy("Clipboard content", npaste=5) # Allowing 5 pastes
```

issue_title: wxPython 4.0 never calls CefApp.OnInit on Mac
issue_body:
The `super(CefApp, self).__init__(redirect=redirect)` call never returns and OnInit is never called. This only occurs if cef.Initialize() was called beforehand.  There seem to be two solutions:

1. Initialize CEF after wx was initialized, eg. during a call to embed_browser(). However that results with autorelease error when exiting app.

2. Implement CefApp.OnPreInit and do wx window creation in that event instead of OnInit.

Issue originally reported on the Forum:
https://groups.google.com/d/topic/cefpython/tM8SXp_a_u0/discussion

issue_title: Extended ID flag ignored for period messages when using socketcan_native
issue_body:
I found that when attempting to send a period message using the 'socketcan_native' interface, the 'extended_id' argument in the constructor for the message is ignored.

I have a small test program to test this : 
```python
#! /usr/bin/env python3

import can
import time

INTERFACE = 'socketcan_native'
CHANNEL = 'vcan0'

can.rc['interface'] = INTERFACE
can.rc['channel'] = CHANNEL

from can.interfaces.interface import Bus

bus = Bus()
msg = can.Message(arbitration_id = 0x12345678,
                  data = [0xAA] * 8,
                  extended_id = True)
period = 1.0

task = (can.send_periodic(CHANNEL, msg, period))

time.sleep(10)

task.stop()
bus.shutdown()
```

The candump output for this is something like : 
```
 (1482224446.405665)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224447.405739)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224448.405804)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224449.405874)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224450.405956)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224451.406010)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224452.406099)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224453.406164)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224454.406242)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224455.406313)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
 (1482224456.406322)  vcan0  678   [8]  AA AA AA AA AA AA AA AA
```
So it looks like the ID is the standard 11 bits only.

When using 'socketcan_ctypes' the id is 12345678 as expected.

I found this can be worked around by setting bit 32 in the arbitration id, so something like : 
```python
arbitration_id= 0x12345678 | (1 << 31)
```

But this should not be necessary right?

I am using Ubuntu 16.04, with Python version 3.5.2

issue_title: Sent messages are being received on the listener
issue_body:
I am using the cyclic example to send a few periodic messages.  I also want to read messages at the same time on the same channel; however, all the messages I am sending are being received on the listener.  Is there a way to disable this?

```
can.rc['interface'] = 'socketcan_native'
can.rc['channel'] = 'can0'
from can.interfaces.interface import Bus
bus = Bus()

msg1 = can.Message(arbitration_id=0x219, extended_id=False, data=[0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0])
msg2 = can.Message(arbitration_id=0x21A, extended_id=False, data=[0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0])

issue_title: Improvement: Support genre tag in the filepath template
issue_body:
I, and [reputedly others](https://www.bogleheads.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=168622) prefer to include genre in our music folder structure. It would be cool if bandcamp-dl supported including the album's genre as part of the filepath template.

I see that bandcamp albums don't have a canonical genre tag, just tags an artist applied to the album. (For discoverability, though, each is likely to include a core genre.) Including all the tags may brush up [against the limits](https://mclowe.bandcamp.com/album/first-of-all-mixtape) of path length. Such a feature would probably only propagate the core genres on bandcamp's landing page. I emphasize core, because artists typically put 'duplicates' with different spellings or hyphenation for SEO.

I propose that a filepath template with _%{genres}_ replace that tag with a comma separated, no-spaces string of the core genre tags. (Slugification removes the commas.) Where an album does not use one of the core genres, bandcamp-dl could substitute _none_ .
* ex: Piraton Record's song [Space Dynamite](https://piratonrecords.bandcamp.com/track/space-dynamite-sand-a) produces _latin,punk,pop_ . It does not include "hip hop" because the canon filter is "hip-hop/rap".

issue_title: Mistletoe plant and logo
issue_body:
Hi,

first of all, thanks for the tool: it's a piece of cake for markdown parsing (and also for custom rendering)!

Just for the sake of precision, I'd like to point out a common misconception about mistletoe, which is not the plant depicted in the logo. The plant of the logo is either the [Ruscus aculeatus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/), also called *butcher's-broom* or *christmas berry* or the [Ilex aquifolium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilex_aquifolium), called *christmas holly*. Instead, the **mistletoe** is the [Viscum album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mistletoe), which has white berries and plain thick leaves (not spiny as the other two plants).

I'd like also to make clear that letting you change the logo is not my purpose, but I want only to friendly make you aware of which is the *“right“* **mistletoe**. :-)

All the best,

Luca

```
from pynput.keyboard import Key, Listener
import os

# get initial capslock state
exit_code = os.system('''xset q 2>/dev/null | grep -q -E 'Caps Lock: +on' ''')
if exit_code == 0:
	capslock = True
else:
	capslock = False

# this function runs each time a key is pressed
def on_press(key): 
	global capslock
	output = str(key)
	if output == 'Key.caps_lock': # if capslock pressed, swap capslock state
		capslock = not capslock
	if output.startswith('Key') == False: # i.e., if output is alphanumeric
		output = output[1:-1] # remove quotes around character
		if capslock:
			output = output.swapcase()
	print(output)

with Listener(on_press=on_press) as listener:
	listener.join()
```

issue_title: Issue with generators
issue_body:
This library doesn't work if there are any generators in the stack as the compiled `raise __traceback_maker` actually executes without raising an error if flag 0x20 (CO_GENERATOR) is set.

Simple repro:
```python
import sys
import tblib
import traceback

def foo():
  raise RuntimeError()
  yield

try:
  next(foo())
except:
  assert traceback.extract_tb(sys.exc_info()[2]) == traceback.extract_tb(
      tblib.Traceback(sys.exc_info()[2]).as_traceback())
```
In general, mucking around with code attributes isn't really safe, and in the worst case could potentially cause a segfault when the code is executed.  The filename and name (the two that actually matter as they are used by the exception printing system) should be safe.  I wouldn't touch nlocals, stacksize, or flags though.  I have seen instances where the overridden nlocals causes the exec itself to throw because there are no locals which screws up the line numbers in the tracebacks.

issue_title: Restrict paths accepted by ConfigManager.get and ConfigManager.set
issue_body:
Valid paths:
 * `('section', 'option')`
 * `('section', 'subsection', 'option')`
 * `('prefix.root.suffix', 'option')`

Tricky paths (valid, but not interpreted as originally):
 * `('section.option')` -- should be interpreted as `c.section == 'DEFAULT'` and `c.option == 'section.option'`.

This is in order to stay closer to standard library's `ConfigParser` and to allow things like domain names in section and option names which is the case in some `ConfigParser` examples in official documentation: https://docs.python.org/3.6/library/configparser.html#quick-start

issue_title: Allow config declaration to be a module, or a dictionary
issue_body:
In #50,  `AppConfig` doesn't have to be a class, and neither do `uploads`, `downloads`, and `other` -- it could be a module or a dictionary:

```
# uploads_config.py
threads = 1

# downloads_config.py
enabled = True

# app_config.py
import uploads_config as uploads
import downloads_config as downloads
greeting = 'Hello'

# app.py
import app_config
config = ConfigManager(app_config)
```

And as a dictionary:
```
app_config = {
    'uploads': {
        'threads': 1,
    },
    'downloads': {
        'enabled': True,
    },
    'greeting': 'Hello',
    'warnings': Item(type=bool),
}

config = ConfigManager(app_config)
```

issue_title: Test issue
issue_body:
# Opening XlsxWriter Issues

## Asking questions about using XlsxWriter

General questions on how to do something with the XlsxWriter module should be
asked on [StackOverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/xlsxwriter).
Add the ``xlsxwriter`` tag to the question. This has a better chance of
getting several answers and also helps others who might have similar questions
in the future.


See below for information on adding a Bug Report or Feature Request.


## Reporting Bugs

Modify the following to suit.


Title: Issue with SOMETHING

Hi,

I am using XlsxWriter to do SOMETHING but it appears to do SOMETHING ELSE.

I am using Python version X.Y.Z and XlsxWriter x.y.z and Excel version X.

Here is some code that demonstrates the problem:

```python

import xlsxwriter

workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook('hello.xlsx')
worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet()

worksheet.write('A1', 'Hello world')

workbook.close()

```

Notes:

1. Ensure that the example code can be run to generate a file that
   demonstrates the issue.
2. Only include code that relates to the issue.
3. Remove non-relevant text from this template.
4. If you are seeing an issue in LibreOffice, OpenOffice or another third
   party application, also test the output with a version of Excel.
5. You can get the required version numbers as follows:

        python --version
        python -c 'import xlsxwriter; print(xlsxwriter.__version__)'


## Feature requests

Add `Feature request:` to the title.

In the comment section describe the feature that you would like to be added.

If you are currently using a workaround you can show that workaround.

issue_title: Handle invalid domain (e.g. ".domain.com")
issue_body:
Around 12:09 UTC, all servers dropped from IRC due to a peer that joined with a hostname in the realname akin to `.domain.com`, and the logs show `ERROR:IRC:encoding with 'idna' codec failed (UnicodeError: label empty or too long)`.

More info on this exception: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25103126/label-empty-or-too-long-python-urllib2

I propose we catch `UnicodeError` and ignore the peer altogether since the hostname is very likely to be invalid and unconnectable to anyone (and might even crash clients).

Maybe we want another `except:` block to account for all exceptions that may be raised by `socket.gethostbyname()`, while we're at it.

issue_title: Support for the 2.0 mysensors / Local Sensor on Gateway
issue_body:
Hi,

It seems that new 2.0 version of mysensors is mostly compatible with the old 1.5 serial protocol, but there's a issue when the gateway also have some local sensors. I was getting a error message saying "Node 0 is unknow, will not add child sensor." when the gateway present his child sensors. My gateway send those messages:

    0;255;3;0;14;Gateway startup complete.
    0;255;3;0;11;My Gateway 
    0;255;3;0;12;1.0
    0;0;0;0;7;
    0;1;0;0;6;
    0;255;3;0;9;No registration required
    0;255;3;0;9;Init complete, id=0, parent=0, distance=0, registration=1
    0;1;1;0;0;28.0
    0;0;1;0;1;59.0 

As I could understand from the code, _handle_presentation was expecting a "present" message with child_id 255 to add the sensor but since it never came the node_id 0 is unknow. I did a ugly hack by adding the sensor at _handle_internal, but may a cleaner solution would be adding the gateway as a sensor at __init__ if the protocol_version is 2.0.

issue_title: Centralized test
issue_body:
MutatorMath has quite good test coverage, but there isn’t a way to run all of the tests at once, so regressions in the interaction of the various files in the library aren’t getting caught. In addition, Travis currently only runs a small subset of the actual tests that are there, and coveralls reports incorrect coverage numbers.

As a start, I propose we keep the doctests that are there but add a mechanism to run them all at once. In the future we may want to cleanup and centralize the tests even further, perhaps converting them to unittest2 or something.

issue_title: py.test seems not to be really supported
issue_body:
I tryed to use flask-fixtures for testing my project with py.test. It turned out that the only way to make fixtures load is to make the test class inherite from unittest.TestCase. Doing so make py.test run tests as unittest tests and act as a test container rather than the actual underlying test lib (it's at least how I understand what's going on here), and thus all the py.test fixture feature is deactivated. It's a huge issue, since I obviously need several fixture from pytest and pytest-flask to test my app (namely the client fixture). The claim that flask-fixtures support py.test seems to rely only on the fact that py.test can run many test lib like unittest. I think the doc should at least be clearer and warn about that, and maybe it would be even better not to claim to support py.test but just indicate that flask-fixtures tests can be run through py.test.

Anyway, I started to look into a way to make flask-fixtures fully support py.test, and if the project maintainer thinks it can be a good addition to the lib, I will go forward on this feature.

issue_title: click() target not an optional argument
issue_body:
In sikulix, `click()` uses the current region's `lastMatch` if no argument is given. Useful for "wait for x and then click it" scenarios and to just let `wait() ` handle wait timeouts:
```
wait('startmenu.png')
click()
```
Lackey's `click()` target argument is not optional though:
```
>>> wait('startmenu.png')
[info] Found match for pattern 'startmenu.png' at (9,1403) with confidence (1.0). Target at (23,1418)
Match[9,1403 29x30] score=1.000000, target=(0, 0)
>>> click()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: click() missing 1 required positional argument: 'target'
```
Looks like the code is already there and just setting the default argument value to `None` fixes this for me.

Note that it then raises a `TypeError` if no `lastMatch` is stored. 
```
>>> click()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "RegionMatching.py", line 568, in click
    raise TypeError("click expected Pattern, String, Match, Region, or Location object")
TypeError: click expected Pattern, String, Match, Region, or Location object
```
Sikuli would just click on the default click point, i.e. the center of the current region/screen.
This only works with lackey if a Region object is explicitly passed as the target (just tested this with `click(Screen(0))`).

issue_title: doesn't work on distributed file system:  should be able to override observer implementation.
issue_body:
Using watchdog on a cluster using IBM General Parallel File System.  The processes writing the files are doing so on one node, and the watchdog is setting the watches on another node.   The inotify events are not gettting to the watcher, so the directory is filling up, and the watch is never triggering.  The current
implementation chooses the observer based on kernel/platform.  Many networks file systems do not support the same facilities as local ones.  I don't think watchdog can fix this, but it should provide the ability to override the platform 'best' observer with other available implementations for cases.

issue_title: Various issues with {Regex,Pattern}MatchingEventHandler
issue_body:
- Currently, full paths are matched to the regexes using `re.match`, which only looks for matches at the beginning of the string.  It would probably make sense to use `re.search` instead, or to match the file name without the directory name.  (This makes it much easier to match actual patterns e.g. `r"^foo\d\d\d\.dat$"` which currently has to be written `r".*/foo\d\d\d\.dat$"` on a POSIX system and `r".*\\foo\d\d\d\.dat$"` on a Windows system.)
- If it is decided that RegexMatchingEventHandler should still match the full path (which is not silly), it would be useful to normalize the path separator to "/" to make cross-platform code easier to write.
- For both Regex and PatternMatchingEventHandler, it would make sense to default case-sensitivity to the platform default (i.e. yes on POSIX, no on Windows).

issue_title: Exception when trying to remove keys that contain an ngram more than once
issue_body:
Attempting this:

    g = ngram.NGram(['aaaa'])
    g.remove('aaaa')

Leads to this exception:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/ngram.py", line 244, in remove
        del self._grams[ngram][item]
    KeyError: 'aaaa'

As far as I can tell this is because `splititem` is used to generate the list of ngrams corresponding to the key. These are then removed from `_grams`, with the intention to keep the set in a consistent state (see [here](https://github.com/gpoulter/python-ngram/blob/master/ngram.py#L243) for details). That all works great until an ngram is encountered the second time. Because the entry was already deleted the first time around it now triggers a `KeyError`.

issue_title: Send and forget pattern can leak connections
issue_body:
Hi Jason,

I am contributing to Elastalert which is an alerting system on the top of Elastic Search. I had a small issue with the way the connected mode is computed. The logic on my side, is that I must send a message to the broker from time to time, so I don't want to keep the connection opened and I simply create a new connection when the alert must be triggered. 

conn = stomp.Connection([(stomp_hostname, stomp_hostport)])
conn.start()
conn.connect(stomp_login, stomp_password)
conn.send(stomp_destination, "hello")
conn.disconnect()

The problem with this code, is that the disconnect code is not called at the end because the connection is not set as CONNECTED because the CONNECTED frame arrives too late. This means that the alert is correctly sent but the connection stays opened on the broker's side. 

Somehow, if you do not send the DISCONNECT frame when the CONNECTED frame is not received, you should refuse to send regular messages as well. The problem with that is that people will have to add extra code to wait for the CONNECTED frame. 

An easy fix would be to set the connected status to true when the connection is opened, so as soon as the socket connection is established.

On my side, I simply added a sleep of 1 second after the connect function which does the trick.

Log example:

**2018-01-19 16:39:40 INFO transport - attempt_connection: Attempting connection to host *******, port 61613
2018-01-19 16:39:40 INFO transport - attempt_connection: Established connection to host *******, port 61613
2018-01-19 16:39:40 INFO transport - __receiver_loop: Starting receiver loop
2018-01-19 16:39:40 DEBUG transport - transmit: Sending frame: ['STOMP', '\n', 'accept-version:1.1\n', 'login:\n', 'passcode:\n', '\n', b'\x00']
2018-01-19 16:39:40 DEBUG transport - transmit: Sending frame: ['SEND', '\n', 'content-length:6\n', 'destination:/queue/ALERT2\n', '\n', b'coucou', b'\x00']
2018-01-19 16:39:40 DEBUG protocol - disconnect: Not sending disconnect, already disconnected**

With a 1 second sleep
**2018-01-19 16:41:02 INFO transport - attempt_connection: Attempting connection to host *******, port 61613
2018-01-19 16:41:02 INFO transport - attempt_connection: Established connection to host *******, port 61613
2018-01-19 16:41:02 INFO transport - __receiver_loop: Starting receiver loop

2018-01-19 16:41:02 DEBUG transport - process_frame: Received frame: 'CONNECTED', headers={'server': 'ActiveMQ/5.15.2', 'heart-beat': '0,0', 'session': 'ID:3edbbf579179-39811-1516265496846-5:916', 'version': '1.1'}, body=''

2018-01-19 16:41:04 DEBUG transport - process_frame: Received frame: 'RECEIPT', headers={'receipt-id': '6b9ac698-be51-4a7a-9178-ca65a25b106a'}, body=''

issue_title: Syntactic suggar for regex-like operators
issue_body:
parglare uses a pure BNF meta-language for grammar specification.
This leads to somewhat verbose grammars. There are a lot of places where a zero-or-more (`*`)  or one-or-more (`+`) regex constructs could be used. parglare should expand these usages to additional productions with a name that is derived from the referenced rule and the regex operation used. The action that is implicitly bounded could be determined also -- e.g. `@collect` for `*` and `+`.

issue_title: local variable 'insecure' referenced before assignment
issue_body:
Hi, 
line 347 of session.py can raise an UnboundLocalError: local variable 'insecure' referenced before assignment

This is because the variable "w" on line 340 can evaluate to True but not contain a match in the "insecure_warnings" list. The "insecure" variable never gets the value True but also never gets the value False since it's in an if...else.

A solution might be to set "insecure" to False then allow it to get the value True if the condition is met.

Matt

issue_body:
Good morning, sir! I am now trying to implement the MSE loss function in PySwarms but I really need some help :( Here is my code...

# Load the dataset
region = "NCR"
filename = region + ".csv"

x = np.array(pd.read_csv(filename, usecols=[1,2,3,4], header=None))
y = np.array(pd.read_csv(filename, usecols=[5], header=None))

normalized_X = preprocessing.scale(x)

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = model_selection.train_test_split(
normalized_X, y, test_size=0.2222222222222222, shuffle=False)

y_train = y_train.ravel()
y_test = y_test.ravel()

# Forward propagation
def forward_prop(params):
    # Neural network architecture
    n_inputs = X_train.shape[1]
    n_hidden = 20
    n_classes = 1

    # Roll-back the weights and biases
    W1 = params[0:80].reshape((n_inputs,n_hidden))
    b1 = params[80:100].reshape((n_hidden,))
    W2 = params[100:120].reshape((n_hidden,n_classes))
    b2 = params[120:121].reshape((n_classes,))

    # Perform forward propagation
    z1 = X_train.dot(W1) + b1  # Pre-activation in Layer 1
    a1 = np.tanh(z1)     # Activation in Layer 1
    z2 = a1.dot(W2) + b2 # Pre-activation in Layer 2
    logits = z2
    
    # Compute for the mean squared error
    N = 84 # Number of samples
    mse = mean_squared_error(y_train, logits)
    loss = np.sum(mse) / N
    return loss

def f(x):
    n_particles = 100
    j = [forward_prop(x[i]) for i in range(n_particles)]
    return np.array(j)

# Initialize swarm
options = {'c1': 0.5, 'c2': 0.5, 'w':0.9}

# Call instance of PSO
dimensions = (4 * 20) + (20 * 1) + 20 + 1
optimizer = ps.single.GlobalBestPSO(n_particles=100, dimensions=dimensions, options=options)

# Perform optimization
cost, pos = optimizer.optimize(f, print_step=100, iters=500, verbose=2)

def predict(X_train, pos):
    # Neural network architecture
    n_inputs = X_test.shape[1]
    n_hidden = 20
    n_classes = 1

    # Roll-back the weights and biases
    W1 = pos[0:80].reshape((n_inputs,n_hidden))
    b1 = pos[80:100].reshape((n_hidden,))
    W2 = pos[100:120].reshape((n_hidden,n_classes))
    b2 = pos[120:121].reshape((n_classes,))

    # Perform forward propagation
    z1 = X_train.dot(W1) + b1  # Pre-activation in Layer 1
    a1 = np.tanh(z1)     # Activation in Layer 1
    z2 = a1.dot(W2) + b2 # Pre-activation in Layer 2
    logits = z2

    y_pred = np.argmax(logits, axis=1)
    print("Actual values:",y_train)
    print("\nPredicted values:",y_pred)
    return y_pred

print("\nPrediction Accuracy:",str(((predict(X_train, pos) == y_train).mean()) * 100) + "%")

issue_title: bugs in handling trailing spaces in numbered params
issue_body:
This is using on branch 'develop' as of early July 16. Trailing spaces in numbered params are not stripped by MediaWiki; therefore, `preserve_spacing` should not operate, but it does.

    >>> x = mp.parser.Parser().parse("{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl}}", skip_style_tags=True)
    >>> x
    u'{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].get("1")
    u'bar '
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].add("1", "bar")
    u'bar '
    >>> x
    u'{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].get("1")
    u'bar '

Even worse, `remove()` appears to sometimes not simply remove the field, but leaves the spaces, and there's no `preserve_spacing` option:

    >>> x = mp.parser.Parser().parse("{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl}}", skip_style_tags=True)
    >>> x
    u'{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].add("2", "bar")
    u'bar'
    >>> x
    u'{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl|bar}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].add("1", "bar")
    u'bar '
    >>> x
    u'{{foo|bar |sc=Cyrl|bar}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].remove("1")
    >>> x
    u'{{foo| |sc=Cyrl|bar}}'
    >>> x.filter_templates()[0].add("1", "bar")
    u' bar'
    >>> x
    u'{{foo| bar|sc=Cyrl|bar}}'

Note how the space was left after removing the field, and then got inserted *before* the newly added param.

issue_title: Windows - Photini won't run after update or reinstallation
issue_body:
If you use Photini on Windows and run the installer or updater to get the latest version it may update the PyQt5 library as well. Unfortunately the latest version of PyQt5 for windows does not include the QtWebEngine (or older QtWebKit) component that Photini needs to display maps.

If Photini doesn't run after updating, try running it in a command window, as described in the documentation: https://photini.readthedocs.io/en/latest/other/installation.html#troubleshooting

If you get a message ending with "ImportError: cannot import name 'QtWebKit'" then you should downgrade PyQt5. Run the command window again, but as administrator, and run the following command:

`pip install PyQt5==5.10.1`

This should install an older version of PyQt5 that is known to work with Photini.

issue_title: Please test upcoming version 0.12rc1
issue_body:
Today I released version 0.12. It supports Django-1.10 and Django-CMS-3.4 and is one major step forward. These are all the changes:

* Added compatibility for Django version 1.10.
* Added compatibility for django-CMS version 3.4.
* Added monkey patch to resolve issues handled by PR https://github.com/divio/django-cms/pull/5809
* Added compatibility for djangocms-text-ckeditor-3.4.
* **Important for AngularJS users**: Please upgrade to angular-ui-bootstrap version 0.14.3. All
  versions later than 0.13 use the prefix ``uib-`` on all AngularJS directives, hence this upgrade
  is required.
* In the ``CarouselSlide`` plugin, caption is added as a child ``TextPlugin`` instead of using the
  glossary. Currently the migration of ``TextLinkPlugins`` inside this caption field does not work
  properly. Please create an issue, if you really need it.
* Added method ``value_omitted_from_data`` to ``JSONMultiWidget`` to override the Django method
  implemented in ``django.forms.widgets.MultiWidget``.
* In ``cmsplugin_cascade.models.CascadeElement`` the foreign key ``shared_glossary`` now is marked
  as editable. Instead to plugins without sharable glossary, the attribute
  ``exclude = ['shared_glossary']`` is added.
* Instead of handling ring.js plugin inheritance through ``get_ring_bases()``, Cascade plugins
  just have to add ``ring_plugin = '...'`` to their class declaration.
* Function ``cmsplugin_cascade.utils.resolve_dependencies`` is deprecated, since Javascript
  dependencies now are handled via their natural inheritance relation.
* The configuration option ``settings.CMSPLUGIN_CASCADE['dependencies']`` has been removed.
* Added method ``save()`` to model ``SharedGlossary``, which filters the glossary to be stored to
  only those fields marked as sharable.
* Accessing the CMS page via ``plugin_instance.page`` is deprecated and has been replaced by
  invocations to ``plugin_instance.placeholder.page``.
* Removed directory ``static/cascade/css/fonts/glyphicons-halflings``, since they are available
  through the Bootstrap npm packages.
* All Javascript files accessing a property ``disabled``, now use the proper jQuery function
  intended for it.
* Added interface to upload fonts and use them as framed icons, text icons or button decorators.
* The permission system now is fine grained. Administrators can give their staff users
  add/change/delete permissions to each of the many Cascade plugins. When adding new plugins, this
  does not even require a database migration.
* Fixed: On saving a **CarouselPlugin**, the glossary of it's children, ie. **CarouselSlidePlugin**,
  is sanitized.
* Handle the high resolution of the **PicturePlugin** through ``srcset`` rather than a ``@media``
  query.
* Handle the high resolution background of the **JumbotronPlugin** through ``image-set`` rather than
  a ``@media`` query.

issue_title: 关于禁用 “词组拼音库” 达到减少内存占用的问题
issue_body:
## 运行环境

* 操作系统（Linux/macOS/Windows）：windows
* Python 版本：2.7.10
* pypinyin 版本：0.25.0


<!--
P.S. 可以通过 `python -V` 获取 Python 版本。  
P.S. 可以通过 `pypinyin -V` 或者 `pip freeze |grep pypinyin` 或 `pypinyin.__version__` 获取 pypinyin 版本信息。
-->

## 问题描述以及解决过程

在我的项目中，汉字转拼音只会转单个的汉字，而不需要词组，如果不对 pypinyin 做任何改动直接导入 pinyin 会占用 30M 左右的内存

```
Line #    Mem usage    Increment   Line Contents
================================================
     7   25.246 MiB   25.246 MiB   @profile
     8                             def func():
     9   52.117 MiB   26.871 MiB       from pypinyin import pinyin,Style
    10   52.125 MiB    0.008 MiB       print pinyin(u'啦', style=Style.INITIALS)

```

通过给 mozillazg 大神发邮件询问，设置环境变量 `PYPINYIN_NO_PHRASES=true` 得以大量的减少了内存占用

设置代码

```python 

import os
os.environ['PYPINYIN_NO_PHRASES'] = 'true'

@profile
def func():
    from pypinyin import pinyin,Style
    print pinyin(u'啦', style=Style.INITIALS)

func()

```

最终的结果

```
Line #    Mem usage    Increment   Line Contents
================================================
     7   25.258 MiB   25.258 MiB   @profile
     8                             def func():
     9   29.176 MiB    3.918 MiB       from pypinyin import pinyin,Style
    10   29.184 MiB    0.008 MiB       print pinyin(u'啦', style=Style.INITIALS)

```

感谢大神的耐心回答，以上是整个问题解决过程，供有相似问题的同学作为参考。


<!--
感谢反馈！❤️
-->

issue_title: Negative quantile approximation with high skew/less data
issue_body:
```
digest = TDigest()
digest.batch_update([62.0, 202.0, 1415.0, 1433.0])
digest.percentile(0.25)
```

Returns `-136.25`. This is because in https://github.com/CamDavidsonPilon/tdigest/blob/master/tdigest/tdigest.py#L166-L167, `delta` is computed as the mean of the means of the neighbouring centroids and is used as the slope to linearly approximate the quantile between the two centroids. In the following line, `m_i + ((p - t) / k - 1/2)*delta` is negative because `delta` is very large, and `p - t = 0` and thus the expression evaluates to `m_i + (-1/2)*delta` which is negative.

issue_title: Nested host resolution
issue_body:
Consider the following `assh.yml`:
```
hosts:
  '*.ec2':
    Comment:
      - AWS EC2 Hosts
      - e.g. i-12345.us-west-1.ec2
    ResolveCommand: /bin/sh -c "aws ec2 describe-instances --region $(echo %h | cut -d '.' -f2) --instance-ids $(echo %h | cut -d '.' -f1) --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].PublicDnsName' | sed -n 2p - | tr -d '[ \"]'"
  bastion:
    HostName: i-12345.us-west-1.ec2
```
(The `ResolveCommand` is pretty ugly - I know 😜)

If I have a machine in us-west-1 with ID `i12345`, I can do `ssh i-12345.us-west-1.ec2` just fine and `assh` will correctly AWS CLI to get the correct public address.

However, if I do `ssh bastion`, `assh` will only resolve it to `i-12345.us-west-1.ec2` and attempts to connect to it directly.

I see that `assh` currently only do one pass of matching, but it would be really nice if `assh` could handle nested host resolution like this. We could have an option named `NestedResolve` that would trigger this behavior on a per-host basis.

Am I missing anything here? Or is there any workaround to achieve my desired results? Thanks a lot.

issue_title: Use __str__ for pretty-printing entries, and __repr__ for object representation
issue_body:
[Documentation for `__repr__`](https://docs.python.org/2/library/functions.html#repr):
> this function makes an attempt to return a string that would yield an object with the same value when passed to eval()

[Documentation for `__str__`](https://docs.python.org/2/library/functions.html#str):
> Return a string containing a nicely printable representation of an object.

Currently, calling `str()` or `print` on a `ChartData` invokes `__repr__` since `__str__` is not implemented.

Instead, `__repr__` could return a representation of the chart object (with corrected dates), and the current code can be used for `__str__`. Maybe the following the behavior:

    > chart = billboard.ChartData('hot-100', date='1996-07-30')
    > print repr(chart)
    billboard.ChartData('hot-100', date='1996-08-03')
    > print chart
    hot-100 chart from 1996-08-03
    -----------------------------
    1. ‘Macarena (Bayside Boys Mix)’ by Los Del Rio
    ...

Should be an easy fix. Let me know if this is a good idea, and I can go ahead and implement it.

issue_title: flask-login: is_authenticated is inconsistent
issue_body:
I have the impression that using is_authenticated from flask_login doesn't work properly. Giving the following logs that I get on my test environment:

    user_id
    True
    2018-06-04 17:05:15,350 - root - INFO - GET - /ngin/static/media/oval.svg? - 304 NOT MODIFIED
    False
    2018-06-04 17:05:15,369 - root - INFO - GET - /ngin/api/v2/menu/main? - 200 OK
    user_id
    True
    2018-06-04 17:05:25,697 - root - INFO - GET - /ngin/static/media/ngin_electricity.svg? - 304 NOT MODIFIED
    False
    2018-06-04 17:05:25,719 - root - INFO - GET - /ngin/api/v2/menu/lvgo? - 200 OK

The log line with the url is in the after_request block, so is printed at the end. The username ('user_id') comes from the login_manager.user_loader ; and the True or False come from the current_user.is_authenticated.
It seems that the user_loader isn't always called. And when it does, the user shows authenticated, but when the call doesn't happen, the is_authenticated returns false. 

My code is structured like this:

    class usrmgmr():

      def __init__(self, app):
        self.login_manager = fl.LoginManager()
        self.login_manager.init_app(app)
        self.login_manager.session_protection = 'strong'
    
        @self.login_manager.user_loader
        def load_user(user_id):
          print(user_id)
          return User(user_id)
    
      #Function to check if a user is authenticated.
      # returns True or False
      def is_loggedin(self):
        return fl.current_user.is_authenticated
    
      #only for debug purposes!
      def do_debug_login(self):
        user = User('user_id')
        fl.login_user(user, remember=True, duration=datetime.timedelta(seconds=7200)) 

And the code is used like this in run.py:

    usrmgmr = login.usrmgmr(app)
    @app.before_request
    def before_every_request():
      #check if for this endpoint a login is required.
      #if yes, and the user is not logged in, force the login method.
      #else we can just move on. Also the user probably is logged in then.
      if msec.needs_login(page_security):
        print(usrmgmr.is_loggedin())
        if usrmgmr.is_loggedin() == False:
           usrmgmr.do_debug_login()

So for every request we check if a user needs to be logged in to see the endpoint, and we check if the user is already logged in.

The strange thing is that sometimes I get 'True' and sometimes I get 'False'. So that doesn't seem very consistent. 

Does anyone know how to fix this, or what the reason is for this behaviour?

issue_title: Settings inheritance
issue_body:
# problem

I would like to have multiple settings files to use according to the environment 'test_settings', 'prod_settings', 'amazon_settings', 'openshift_settings' etc... but I would like the ability to mix then

Currently I can use the worst solution ever

```python
try:
    from local_settings import *
except ImportError:
    pass
```

But, it is usual to avoid using "imports" inside settings file, that kind of file should be a simple repository of **KEY:value**, besides that, there is the fact that the hierarchy order can change between environments.

# solution

So in order to do have this I propose this **SSEP** (Simple Settings Enhancement Proposal) LOL

having 3 settings files in project 

    settings.py
    test_settings.cfg
    vendor/amazon_settings.py

```bash
python myproject.py --settings=settings,test_settings.cfg,vendor/amazon_settings.py
```

1. can accept only the name of settings module, without the need to specify the file extensions
2. Settings can reside in **.py** or in **.cfg** files
3. can accept full directory path
4. Load the settings in the specified order, overriding the conflicted keys (dict.update automatically does that)

# caveats

Maybe it is not simple settings anymore! LOL

issue_title: Support xlsm file type
issue_body:
In its current state openpyxl-templates doesn't support xlsm files.
It's able to correctly open a xlsm file, replace its content but when saving all the VBA macro are lost.
If the file has still the xlsm extension, then Excel will fail to open it.
When the file is renamed to .xslx, Excel can open it but the macro are missing.

A solution is to use the `keep_vba=True` parameters in the load_workbook method.
Then when saving the file all VBA macros will be added back and it will correctly open in Excel.

issue_title: Client sending a PING packet to a closed socket raises IOError
issue_body:
Good day to you!
I have a question concerning the problem in the title.
What I believe is happening and why does a server gets a PING packet from a disconnected user:
1. Client sends a lot of requests through socket.
2. Client's browser gets stuck on some request and client's socket misses sending a PING packet
3. Server closes a socket because of the timeout when trying to send a response to a request sent earlier (Socket.py, line 63, I get "Client is gone, closing socket")
4. Client sends it's PING packet at pretty much the same time
5. Server gets the PING packet and automatically tries to send a PONG, without checking that socket is closed (Socket.py, line 47)
6. In send function (Socket.py, line 58) there is a check that socket is closed -> raises IOError

My questions are:
- Should a Socket.py contain a check before sending a PONG or is it intended to be this way?
- If latter: is there any place I can add a check for PING packet that socket is closed myself? (without changing a library itself)

I hope the problem is clear.
If it will be necessary, I believe I can provide a log for that

issue_title: Publish/subscribe leaves server running
issue_body:
I would imagine the below code should shutdown the server after it's done. Instead the server process is left running. Leaving out either the subscribe or the publish results in the server shutting down cleanly.
~~~~
import redislite
r = redislite.StrictRedis()
p = r.pubsub()
p.subscribe("chan")
r.publish("chan", "")
p.close()
~~~~

(Tested with version 3.2.311)

issue_title: wrong shape after using concat when using HybridBlock
issue_body:
```python
import mxnet as mx
from mxnet import gluon
from mxnet.gluon import nn

class TestNet(gluon.HybridBlock):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super(TestNet, self).__init__(**kwargs)
        with self.name_scope():
            self.fc = nn.Dense(128)

    def hybrid_forward(self, F, x):
        print "hybrid: origin shape of x is {}".format(x.shape)
        y = F.concat(self.fc(x), self.fc(x))
        print "hybrid: change shape of x to {}".format(y.shape)
        return self.fc(x)

net = TestNet()
net.collect_params().initialize()
x = mx.nd.normal(shape=(10,103))
net(x)

print "ndarray: shape of x is {}".format(mx.nd.concat(x,x).shape)
```

result:  
```shell
hybrid: origin shape of x is (10L, 103L)
hybrid: change shape of x to (10L, 256L)
ndarray: shape of x is (10L, 206L)
```

why the shape changed is (10, 256) and not (10, 206)?  @ZiyueHuang

issue_title: test_operator_gpu.test_deformable_psroipooling_with_type has fixed seed that can mask flakiness
issue_body:
The unit test in title have been using fixed seed to mask flakiness. Suggested action:
1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.
